examples.title: TCP Stop-and-Wait Sender
examples.description: Demonstrates basic Stop-and-Wait ARQ protocol logic
examples.code:
PROCEDURE StopAndWaitSender
  SET packetNumber = 0
  WHILE dataRemaining DO
    SEND packet[ packetNumber ]
    START timer
    WAIT FOR ACK
    IF ACK_received THEN
      packetNumber = packetNumber + 1
      STOP timer
    ELSE IF timeout THEN
      // Retransmit same packet
      RESEND packet[ packetNumber ]
      RESTART timer
    END IF
  END WHILE
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Implements Stop-and-Wait ARQ: sends one packet, waits for ACK. If timeout occurs without ACK, retransmits same packet. Simple but inefficient.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for n packets (worst case n retransmissions per packet)

examples.title: TCP Sliding Window Receiver
examples.description: Demonstrates receiver-side sliding window with buffering
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SlidingWindowReceiver
  SET windowStart = 0
  SET expectedSeqNum = 0
  INITIALIZE receiveBuffer[ windowSize ]
  
  WHILE connectionActive DO
    WAIT FOR packet
    IF packet.seqNum == expectedSeqNum THEN
      DELIVER data to application
      expectedSeqNum = expectedSeqNum + packet.length
      // Deliver any buffered in-order packets
      WHILE receiveBuffer[ expectedSeqNum ] exists DO
        DELIVER receiveBuffer[ expectedSeqNum ]
        DELETE receiveBuffer[ expectedSeqNum ]
        expectedSeqNum = expectedSeqNum + 1
      END WHILE
      SEND ACK( expectedSeqNum )
    ELSE IF packet.seqNum > expectedSeqNum THEN
      // Out-of-order packet, buffer it
      STORE packet in receiveBuffer[ packet.seqNum ]
      SEND ACK( expectedSeqNum )  // Duplicate ACK
    ELSE
      // Duplicate old packet
      SEND ACK( expectedSeqNum )
    END IF
  END WHILE
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Handles incoming packets, delivers in-order data to application, buffers out-of-order packets, sends cumulative ACKs indicating next expected byte.

examples.title: TCP Flow Control Sender
examples.description: Demonstrates sender-side flow control using advertised window
examples.code:
PROCEDURE FlowControlSender
  SET sendBase = 0        // Oldest unacknowledged byte
  SET nextSeqNum = 0      // Next byte to send
  SET windowSize = advertisedWindow
  
  WHILE dataRemaining DO
    // Wait for window to open if full
    WHILE (nextSeqNum - sendBase) >= windowSize DO
      WAIT FOR ACK  // This will update window
    END WHILE
    
    // Send packet if within window
    IF nextSeqNum < sendBase + windowSize THEN
      SEND packet with seqNum = nextSeqNum
      START timer IF this is the oldest unacknowledged
      nextSeqNum = nextSeqNum + packetSize
    END IF
    
    // Process incoming ACKs
    IF ACK_received THEN
      IF ackNum > sendBase THEN
        sendBase = ackNum
        STOP/CANCEL timer for acknowledged data
      END IF
      windowSize = receivedWindowSize  // Update from receiver
    END IF
  END WHILE
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Implements flow control by limiting unacknowledged data to advertised window size. Updates window based on receiver's advertisements and ACKs.

examples.title: Python UDP Socket - No Flow Control
examples.description: Demonstrates UDP's connectionless nature and lack of flow control
examples.code:
import socket

# UDP Server
server_socket = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_DGRAM)
server_socket.bind(('127.0.0.1', 8888))

while True:
    # Receive data without any flow control
    message, client_address = server_socket.recvfrom(1024)
    print(f"Received {len(message)} bytes from {client_address}")
    
    # Process and respond - no guarantee client receives it
    response = b"ACK" + message
    server_socket.sendto(response, client_address)

# UDP Client
client_socket = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_DGRAM)
message = b"Hello UDP"
# Send without establishing connection or checking receiver capacity
client_socket.sendto(message, ('127.0.0.1', 8888))

# No flow control - data sent immediately without receiver's permission
examples.explanation: Shows UDP's simplicity: no connection setup (bind/connect), no window advertisements, no acknowledgments. Sender transmits without checking if receiver can handle it.
